justification.personality: The book shows him as an active, participating member of the student group (Scenes 1, 11, 12, 13, 14, 15, 17, 18). The film only shows him as part of a background group passing the Portkey (Film Scene 1). His specific, witty, and knowledgeable interactions are entirely absent.
justification.narrative_role: In the books, he is a recurring, minor character who adds flavor and specific interactions (e.g., questioning the Basilisk, asking about Protean Charm, participating in the Portkey group). In the film, he is reduced to a generic background extra in the Portkey scene, losing all narrative function.
justification.motivations: His motivations in the books are simply participation and curiosity regarding magical artifacts/skills. The film does not give him any discernible motivation or conflict, reducing him to a passive background element.
justification.character_arc: Since his appearances are sporadic and his function is purely additive in the books, the film's failure to include his specific interactions means his arc (or lack thereof) is not reflected. He has no discernible arc in either medium, but the film fails to capture his presence meaningfully.

comparative.key_observations: Terry Boot is a very minor character whose primary function in the books is to add flavor and specific, witty dialogue. The film adaptation completely omits almost all of his unique interactions, reducing him to a non-speaking background extra.
